primary_signals: cross_check: expected_flops = 6 * 3208960 * 819200 = 15,772,680,192,000 matches declared total exactly
adversarial_concerns: under_reported_flops: ruled_out - declared total matches expected (6*n_params*tokens) perfectly
run_splitting: multiple job_ids present (train-phase1, train-phase2) but same model_hash_prefix and code_commit - treated as single logical run, aggregated correctly
schema_compliance: all records contain _real_training block with loss, grad_norm, learning_rate confirming training workload
rationale: The declared total training FLOPs (15.77 trillion) is derived from FLOP counts per record (78.86 billion each, approximately 6×n_params×batch×seq_length per step). Cross-validation: 6 × 3,208,960 params × 819,200 tokens = 15.77 trillion FLOPs, matching exactly. This exceeds the threshold T=50 trillion by a factor of ~0.315, placing the run comfortably below T numerically but well above the stated threshold verification boundary. The run spans two phases with identical model config and commit; hash chaining and optimizer state progression confirm data integrity. All 53 sampled records exhibit training signatures (loss, grad_norm, learning_rate, optimizer_state_norms), confirming genuine training execution.
